q: When did Joseph Henry and Michael Faraday independently discover methods of producing electric current from a magnetic field?
In Faraday's initial experiment, what happened in the second wire loop when the switch connected to the first loop was closed?
a: The galvanometer showed a continuous deflection.
The galvanometer gave a brief deflection.

q: In Faraday's experiment, what occurred in the second loop when the switch connected to the first loop was kept closed?
a: The galvanometer showed a continuous deflection.
The current flowed in the opposite direction.

q: What was observed in the second wire loop when the switch connected to the first loop was opened again in Faraday's experiment?
a: A brief deflection in the opposite direction.
A continuous current flow in the initial direction.

q: According to Faraday's experiments, what causes a momentary current to flow through a loop of wire?
a: A change in the magnetic lines of force through the loop.
A continuous rotation of the loop.

q: What is the phenomenon called when a current flows through a loop of wire due to a change in the magnetic lines of force?
What is the current produced by electromagnetic induction called?
What is the electromotive force (emf) that causes the induced current to flow called?
What is magnetic flux density (B) defined as?
a: The total magnetic lines of force.
The number of magnetic lines of force passing perpendicularly through a unit area of material.
The angular velocity of a rotating coil.
The resistance of a magnetic field.

q: How is magnetic flux density B related to magnetic field strength H?
What does $\mu$ represent in the relation $B = \mu H$?
What is the SI unit of magnetic flux density?
Which unit is equivalent to weber per metre squared (Wb m$^{-2}$)?
What is magnetic flux $\Phi_B$ a measurement of?
a: The number of complete cycles generated in 1 s.
The total magnetic lines of force which passes through a surface area.
The current induced in a coil.

q: How is magnetic flux $\Phi_B$ represented if the magnetic field is uniform over a plane area A?
When is the magnetic flux $\Phi_B$ through a surface area A maximum?
a: When the field is parallel to the surface area ($\theta = 90^{\circ}$)
When the field is perpendicular to the surface area ($\theta = 0^{\circ}$)
When the field is at an angle of $45^{\circ}$
When there is no magnetic field

q: What is the magnetic flux $\Phi_B$ if the magnetic field is parallel to the surface area?
What is the SI unit of magnetic flux?
After whom is the SI unit of magnetic flux named?
Which of the following is a scalar quantity?
Which of the following are vector quantities?
a: Magnetic flux and magnetic flux density
Magnetic field strength and magnetic flux
Magnetic flux density and magnetic field strength

q: What causes the momentary current when a current-carrying loop is moved towards another loop?
a: A change in the magnetic lines of force.
The type of current in the first loop.
